mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits an easing shock through lower discount rates and credit conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month when the policy rate is in decline.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a declining FEDFUNDS trend, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and lower future earnings, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal has no negative average return spread versus untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while CPIAUCSL is stable, it indicates real consumer demand is holding up without immediate inflation pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to outperform either series alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in the top tercile of RSAFS growth conditional on low CPIAUCSL acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects stronger industrial production and cyclical earnings momentum, which should support equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO increases are followed by nonpositive SPX 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses significance after controlling for the market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST indicates collapsing residential demand and a broader slowdown in interest-sensitive spending, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if year-over-year HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL signals restrictive real policy, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-rate/high-inflation condition does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either proxy alone, or if triggered returns are not negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth and a rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration that should weaken earnings expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the following week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month over month while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration signal does not lead to negative 5-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle slowdown, weak GDP growth together with falling retail sales indicates demand exhaustion, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-growth/weak-sales condition fails to generate negative 60-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the signal is not significant in rolling out-of-sample tests.
